rulings.Case Title: VALERIE ANN CARLING et al VS. ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment: hearing required. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DALE RIGG et al VS. ASTENJOHNSON, INC., 'F/K/A ASTEN INC et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Briggs & Stratton Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to plaintiff Dale Rigg and granted as to plaintiff Susan Rigg. The deposition testimony of Dr. Dichmann and the declaration of Mr. Lipari create a triable issue whether Mr. Rigg was permanently precluded from performing his regular occupation as a result of mesothelioma more than a year prior to the filing of the complaint. The objections to Mr. Lipari¿s declaration are overruled. Plaintiff Susan Rigg was aware that her husband had mesothelioma in 2006 and CCP 340.2 does not apply to loss of consortium claims. (Uram v. Abex Corp. (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 1425). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN AYER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Crane Co.¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to exposure (issues 1-4), false representation (issue 5), and intentional tort (issue 6), and granted as to punitive damages (issue 7). Mr. Throne¿s declaration presents a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos in original packing attributable to Crane Co. Crane Co.¿s request for a continuance of the hearing in order to complete Mr. Throne¿s deposition is denied. Crane Co.¿s objections to Mr. Throne¿s declaration are overruled. Crane Co. failed to shift the burden on the claims for false representation and intentional tort because it failed to propound any ¿all facts¿ interrogatories on these claims and did not ask plaintiff any questions about these claims at his deposition. Crane Co. satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to create a triable issue whether Crane Co. acted with fraud, malice, or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. Because the failure of plaintiff¿s counsel to cite page and line numbers in their evidence in opposition to Crane Co.¿s motion contravenes CRC 3.1350(f) and imposes additional burdens on the court, plaintiffs¿ counsel is admonished to do so in the future. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN AYER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Sepco Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff raised a triable issue of exposure to an asbestos-containing product attributable to Sepco through the declaration of Mr. Throne and the prior deposition testimony of Mr. Gibson wherein he testified that 99% of all gaskets sold by Sepco Corporation contained chrysotile. Sepco¿s objections to Mr. Throne¿s declaration are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN AYER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Schneider Electrical USA, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff¿s discovery responses are factually devoid with respect to the asbestos content of the defendant¿s products. Plaintiff¿s evidence fails to create a triable issue on the question of whether the Square D products plaintiff worked with contained asbestos. =(220/HEK)
